Basic HTML Structure

A test document demonstrating standard HTML5 semantic elements.

  • Semantic Elements
  • Grouping
  • Interactive

Semantic Elements

Article Element

The <article> element represents self-contained content that could be independently distributed.

Published: January 15, 2025

Aside Element

This is tangentially related content, typically used for sidebars or callout boxes.

Grouping Content

Address Element

Contact: test@example.com
123 Test Street, Example City

Blockquote

This is a blockquote element with a citation attribute.

Preformatted Text

function hello() {
  console.log("Hello, world!");
}

Horizontal Rule

Content above the rule.

Content below the rule.

Interactive Elements

Click to expand this section

This content is hidden by default and revealed when the user clicks the summary.

  • Item one
  • Item two
  • Item three
This section is open by default

The open attribute makes this details element visible initially.

Dialog Reference

The <dialog> element can be used for modal dialogs (requires JavaScript to open).

This is an inline dialog element with the open attribute.
